items.title: Spotify Confirms Streaming Fraud After Kalshi Trader Flags Suspicious Malcolm Todd Numbers
items.summary: Tuesday, Malcolm Todd — the Steve Lacy-esque alt-pop artist and brother to fellow rising star Audrey Hobert — was awarded his first #1 song on Spotify when "Earrings" topped the Global Daily singles chart with 4.165 million streams. However, after a Kalshi trader noticed something suspicious about the numbers, the streaming service confirmed fraud and deleted more than 500,000 of the song's streams, leaving it at #4.

items.title: Horse Feathers Announce Words Are Dead 20th Anniversary Reissue & Tour
items.summary: Back in 2006, Horse Feathers were making a lot of noise, metaphorically speaking. The Portland band released their debut album Words Are Dead right between the freak folk craze and the emergence of more polished indie-folk superstars like Fleet Foxes and Bon Iver, and their music could be understood as a bridge between those phases.

items.title: Living Weapon Announce Debut Album Death In The Family: Hear “The Leaving Process”
items.summary: Living Weapon came crashing into the Boston metalcore scene with their 2021 Paradise EP. Now the band — which is fronted by Vein.fm bassist/backing vocalist Jonathan Lhaubouet — is announcing their full-length debut, Death In The Family. The post Living Weapon Announce Debut Album <em>Death In The Family</em>: Hear “The Leaving Process” appeared first on Stereogum.
